title: Strolid Marketing Execution & Approval Process (VP + DMS + CEO)
summary: This document outlines Strolid's marketing execution and approval process, defining clear roles for the VP of Marketing, Digital Marketing Strategist (DMS), and CEO/CRO to streamline content planning, creation, and approval. It details weekly cadences, approval flows for various content types, and tracking mechanisms to ensure consistent and efficient marketing operations.
text:
1)  One-Page  SOP  
“Marketing  Execution  &  Approval  Process”  
You  can  title  the  doc:  
Strolid  Marketing  Execution  &  Approval  Process  (VP  +  DMS  +  CEO)  
 
1.  Purpose  
To  give  the  Digital  Marketing  Strategist  (DMS)  a  clear,  repeatable  system  to  plan,  create,  and  
ship
 
content
 
—
 
and
 
to
 
give
 
the
 
VP
 
of
 
Marketing
 
and
 
CEO
 
a
 
consistent
 
way
 
to
 
guide
 
and
 
approve
 
work
 
without
 
chaos
 
or
 
yelling.
 
 
2.  Roles  
VP  of  Marketing  –  Strategy  &  Coaching  (“What  &  Why”)  
●  Owns  marketing  strategy,  priorities,  and  monthly/quarterly  themes.  
 ●  Aligns  campaigns  with  company  goals  (brand,  pipeline,  recruiting,  etc.).  
 ●  Reviews  performance  and  provides  constructive  feedback  and  coaching.  
 ●  Ensures  the  DMS  understands  expectations,  standards,  and  priorities.  
 
Digital  Marketing  Strategist  (DMS)  –  Execution  (“How  &  Done”)  
●  Translates  strategy  into  content  and  deliverables.  Responsible  for:  
 ○  3  posts  per  week  +  Friday  carousel  summarizing  the  week.  
 ○  Creative  thumbnails  and  images  to  support  those  posts.  
 ○  Newsletter  (working  with  VP  of  Operations  +  Director  of  HR).  
 ○  Podcast  editing  and  creation  of  short  clips  for  social  once  launched.  
○  Bi-weekly  email  campaigns  to  coincide  with  recent  themes  
 ○  Press  release  drafts  and  digital  distribution  assets.  
 ○  Website  content  updates  and  starting  a  steady  blog  cadence.  
 ●  Maintains  the  content  calendar  and  status  board  (see  Section  5).  
 ●  Delivers  content  drafts  on  time  for  approval.  
 
CEO/CRO  –  Content  Approval  (Copy  Only)  
●  Reviews  and  approves  content/copy  only  (no  creative  design)  on  a  fixed  schedule.  
 ●  Provides  clear  edits  and  comments  inside  Google  Docs .  
 ●  Final  call  on  messaging,  voice,  and  any  sensitive  topics.  
 
 
3.  Approval  Process  (CEO/CRO)  
Scope:  
●  Social  post  copy  (3  weekly  posts  +  Friday  carousel  copy).  
 ●  Newsletter  copy  (VP  of  operations/HR  Director).  
 ●  Podcast  titles,  descriptions,  and  promo  post  copy.  ●  Bi  weekly  email  campaigns  
 ●  PR  /  announcement  copy.  
 ●  Blog  and  core  website  copy.  
 
Process:  
1.  The  DMS  drafts  content  in  Google  Docs  and  shares  it  in  a  clearly  labeled  folder  (e.g.,  Marketing  >  Weekly  Content  >  Week  of  [Date]).  
 
2.  All  content  for  the  following  week  must  be  drafted  and  ready  for  review  by  Thursday  
EOD
 
by
 
CEO
 
and
 
CRO.
 
 3.  The  CEO/CRO  will  review  and  approve  (or  edit)  content  on  Thursday,  or  by  Friday  
at
 
the
 
latest,
 
of
 
the
 
prior
 
week
.
 
 4.  After  review:  
 ○  The  DMS  incorporates  edits.  
 ○  Items  move  to  “Approved  –  Ready  for  Creative  /  Scheduling.”  
 ○  Creative  assets  (thumbnails,  carousels,  images)  are  finalized  and  posts  
scheduled.
 
 
Note:  CEO/CRO  approval  is  for  words,  messaging,  and  positioning  —  not  for  visual  
design
 
but
 
may
 
ask
 
for
 
changes
 
to
 
visual.
 
 
4.  Weekly  Cadence  (DMS)  
Core  Weekly  Goals:  
●  3  posts  +  1  carousel.  
 ●  Progress  on  podcast,  newsletter,  PR,  blogs,  and  website  updates.  
 ●  All  next-week  content  ready  for  Thu/Fri  review.  
 
Suggested  Weekly  Flow  
●  Monday  
 ○  Plan  topics  for  the  following  week’s  posts  (aligned  to  strategy/theme).  
 ○  Draft  next-week  copy  for  social  posts  and  Friday’s  carousel  in  Google  Docs.  ○  Optional  (prep  for  weeks  ahead   
 ●  Tuesday  
 ○  Work  on  long-form:  blogs,  website  copy,  or  PR  drafts.  
 
○  Review  last  week’s  performance  and  summarize  key  learnings.  
 ●  Wednesday  
 ○  Podcast  (if  scheduled  and  ready)  and  begin  editing.  
 ○  Identify  clips  for  shorts.  
 ●  Thursday  
 ○  Finalize  all  next-week  drafts  in  Google  Docs.  
 ○  Move  items  to  “Awaiting  CEO  Review.”  
 ●  Friday  
 ○  Publish  current  week’s  final  post  +  carousel.  
 ○  CEO  reviews  and  edits  next-week  content  (Thu/Fri).  
 ○  DMS  applies  edits,  moves  items  to  “Approved  –  Ready  to  Schedule.”  
 ○  VP  +  DMS  weekly  sync:  review  what  shipped,  performance,  and  next  week.  
 
 
5.  Newsletter  Workflow  
Cadence:  [To  be  decided  by  VP  of  Operations  –  e.g.,  monthly  /  bi-weekly  /  quarterly]  
Stakeholders:  DMS  of  Marketing,  VP  of  Operations,  DMS  of  HR  
Process:  
1.  DMS  requests  content  inputs  from  VP  Ops  &  HR  (wins,  people  updates,  key  messages,  
events).
 
 2.  DMS  drafts  newsletter  in  Google  Docs.  
 3.  VP  Ops  +  HR  review  and  comment.  
 4.  Newsletter  draft  is  included  in  the  CEO’s  standard  Thu/Fri  review  window.  
 
5.  After  approval,  the  DMS  sends  via  email  platform  and  logs  basic  performance  metrics.  
 
 
6.  Other  Workstreams  (Same  Approval  Flow)  
●  Podcast:  
 ○  DMS  drafts  episode  titles,  descriptions,  and  promo  post  copy  →  CEO  reviews  
Thu/Fri
 
→
 
DMS
 
publishes
 
and
 
promotes.
 ●  By  weekly  Email  campaigns:  ○  DMS  drafts  email  based  on  current  Strolid  These  ●  Press  Releases:  
 ○  If  not  frequent  but  requires  the  DMS  to  draft  PR  in  Google  Docs  →  CEO/CRO  
reviews
 
Thu/Fri
 
→
 
DMS
 
finalizes
 
distribution
 
and
 
supporting
 
posts.
 
 ●  Website  &  Blogs:  
 ○  Draft  pages/posts  in  Google  Docs  →  CEO/CRO  review  Thu/Fri  →  DMS  
publishes
 
and
 
tracks
 
performance.
 
 
 
7.  Tracking  &  Transparency  
The  DMS  maintains  a  simple  board/sheet  with  these  statuses:  
●  Backlog  /  Ideas  
 ●  Drafting  
 ●  Awaiting  CEO/CRO  Review  (Thu/Fri)  
 ●  Approved  –  Ready  for  Creative  /  Scheduling  
 ●  Scheduled  
 ●  Published  (with  link  +  basic  results)  
 
This  gives:  
●  The  DMS:  clarity  on  what  to  work  on.  
 ●  The  VP:  visibility  into  the  pipeline.  
 ●  The  CEO/CRO:  a  clean  weekly  approval  touchpoint.  
 
 
2)  Notion  Board  /  Google  Sheet  Structure  
You  can  paste  this  table  into  Notion  or  Sheets  and  use  it  as  your  content  pipeline  tracker.  
Table  Columns  
Item  Name  
Type  Week  Of  
Owner  
Status  Due  Date  
CEO  Review  Week  
Doc  Link  
Channel(s)  
Notes  
Example:  “Post  –  Service  BDC  Tip”  
Social  Post  
2025-11-24  
DMS  Drafting  
2025-11-20  
2025-11-17  
link  to  Google  Doc  
LinkedIn  /  Email  
Part  of  weekly  carousel  
          
Recommended  values:  
●  Type:  
 ○  Social  Post  
 ○  Carousel  
 ○  Newsletter  ○  Email  campaigns   ○  Podcast  –  Full  Episode  
 ○  Podcast  –  Short  Clip  
 ○  Press  Release  
 ○  Blog  Post  
 
○  Website  Page  
 ●  Status:  
 ○  Backlog  /  Idea  
 ○  Drafting  
 ○  Awaiting  CEO/CRO  Review  
 ○  CEO/CRO  Changes  Applied  
 ○  Approved  –  Ready  for  Creative  
 ○  Scheduled  
 ○  Published  
 ●  Owner:  usually  the  DMS,  but  could  be  another  marketer  later.  
 
We  can  also  add  or  use  (Zee  has  a  version)  a  separate  small  table  for  weekly  posts  if  we  want  
it
 
very
